key_insight: The paper uses TLA+ and Verus to formally define and verify four concurrency anomalies in multi-agent LLM runtimes, then proves a strict hierarchy of consistency levels and runtime refinements that prevent them.
engineering_relevance: It gives system builders verified detectors and prevention mechanisms for shared-memory, vector-index, and tool-registry races in durable, deterministic multi-agent LLM workflows.
method: Modeled shared-state LLM interactions as deterministic read-generate-write operations in TLA+ and extracted anomaly counterexamples with TLC.
Built 274 Verus obligations to prove detector soundness/completeness and runtime refinement against a small trust base.
Implemented and verified prevention variants for multiple runtime levels, including locking, snapshot isolation, and commit-order sequencing.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 0/1000 failures for dependency-free prevention twins versus 1000/1000 for the corresponding anomalies, plus live evaluation of one anomaly across three model families with 120/120 prevention in retracted sessions.
watch_item: The guarantees depend on deterministic replay semantics and a small set of structural axioms, so integration with nondeterministic tools or unmodeled runtime behaviors may require additional verification.
paper_title: Verified Detection and Prevention of Concurrency Anomalies in Multi-Agent Large Language Model Systems
